summary: Backend-only verification completed for plugin licensing integration using static code review plus pytest execution. Endpoint runtime validation against public URL could not run because NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and MOREPHI_PUBLIC_CLIENT_TOKEN were not configured in this environment.

critical.issue: Certificate signing falls back to a deterministic private key derived from JWT_SECRET when LICENSE_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY_PEM is absent. This is a security risk for production signing-key management.

minor.issue: Payload includes product_id but endpoint does not validate product_id against activation/license context.

Coverage currently only checks missing token and unknown license paths; no runtime assertion for successful activate/refresh response schema (certificate_payload/signature/public_key_id) or deactivation success path.
All plugin/purchasing pytest cases were skipped due to missing NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL/REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL and/or MOREPHI_PUBLIC_CLIENT_TOKEN in this container context.

action_items: Provide public runtime env values (NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL or REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, and MOREPHI_PUBLIC_CLIENT_TOKEN) and rerun plugin API tests against live preview endpoint.
Replace deterministic JWT-derived signing fallback with explicit managed Ed25519 key provisioning for production.
Add plugin API positive-path tests covering activate, refresh, and deactivate response/body validation.
critical_code_review_comments: Plugin signing key lifecycle is coupled to JWT secret fallback; separate signing key management is required for secure key rotation and blast-radius isolation.
C++ compatibility mapping looks correct: ActivationClient endpoints/path updates and certificate field parsing align with backend response shape; MPHI and MPH1 parsing coexist as intended.

success_rate.backend: 60% (static verification complete; runtime endpoint checks skipped due to missing env)

test_credentials: Read: /app/memory/test_credentials.md (no private creds provided for this integration).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pytest ran successfully but skipped runtime API cases because BASE_URL/token envs were missing. Focus next run on live endpoint validation of plugin activate/refresh/deactivate including certificate payload/signature/public_key_id contract and C++ verifier compatibility.
rca of the issue: Primary blocker RCA: runtime tests depend on env-injected public URL and plugin token; these were not present, so pytest fixtures intentionally skipped cases. Security RCA: signing_key() fallback creates deterministic Ed25519 key from JWT_SECRET, which can undermine signing-key isolation and rotation strategy in production.
